nodes.label: P04_T001
nodes.identity_summary: This person is a price-conscious convenience seeker who prioritizes how others perceive them over product sophistication. They trust expert recommendations and quick commerce delivery more than brand loyalty, frequently switching between affordable options (₹100-200 range) based on discounts and availability. Their purchase decisions are driven by immediate social perception needs rather than long-term brand relationships.

nodes.label: price-caps-at-200-rupees-for-routine-purchases
nodes.description: Consistently stays within ₹100-200 range for body wash, viewing anything above ₹300 as expensive and requiring special justification.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 100_200
[SYNTHETIC] C) Buy three budget body washes at ₹150-170 each and keep some change. I'm not particular about body wash requirements - any decent product works for me, so why spend more?
[SYNTHETIC] ₹549 is way above my usual ₹100-200 range
[REAL] Paid ₹250-300 for a Lux premium body wash. Didn't feel any different from sub-₹100 Nivea and other brands
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium body wash concept above ₹300, this person would likely dismiss it immediately or look for cheaper alternatives because their mental price ceiling is firmly set at ₹200.

nodes.label: switches-brands-for-discounts-over-loyalty
nodes.description: Has moderate brand loyalty but readily switches when better deals appear, frequently ending up with different brands based on price and availability.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Moderate_loyalty
[REAL] No particular reason – there was a discount on Blinkit, so I tried the brand
[SYNTHETIC] C) Search for my usual brand on another app to see if it's cheaper there
[REAL] No favorite brands, but I frequently end up using Lux and Nivea
nodes.predictive_value: When shown competing body wash offers, this person would likely choose based on current pricing and deals rather than brand preference, making them highly responsive to promotional campaigns.

nodes.label: fragrance-for-others-perception-priority
nodes.description: Views body wash fragrance primarily as a tool for positive social perception rather than personal enjoyment, caring deeply about how others perceive their scent.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] It's about how others perceive me and whether they have a positive feeling after interacting with me. If I don't smell good, it negatively impacts their perception
[REAL] A fragrance feels special if it lasts longer and signals freshly bathed
[SYNTHETIC] C) Not try it — fragrance is non-negotiable in body wash. I care about how I smell to others, so an unscented body wash defeats the purpose for me
[REAL] The only reason I would pay 2x my current price is if people notice that I have changed body washes and they are impressed by the fragrance
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash ad emphasizing long-lasting fragrance and social appeal, this person would likely be highly interested because fragrance directly impacts their social confidence.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-dependency-for-instant-needs
nodes.description: Heavily relies on quick commerce apps like Blinkit for instant delivery when running out of products, prioritizing convenience over selection.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Yes, I heavily use Blinkit, Swiggy, Instamart for personal care – instant delivery whenever I run out. Usually satisfied with the selection; enough options to choose from
[REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ['quick_commerce','amazon','social_commerce']
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Blinkit and get it delivered in 15 minutes. I heavily use quick commerce whenever I run out
[SYNTHETIC] I heavily use quick commerce for instant delivery when I run out
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash launch, this person would likely expect it to be available on quick commerce platforms and would be frustrated if they had to wait for traditional delivery when they need it urgently.

nodes.label: dermatologist-recommendations-trump-all-sources
nodes.description: Places dermatologists as the highest trust source (rating 5/5) for personal care advice, significantly above friends, family, or online reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {'Dermatologists or skin doctors':5,'Friends and family':4,'Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)':3,'TV advertisements':3,'Instagram or YouTube influencers':2,'Claims written on the product packaging':1}
[REAL] Would you prefer a product that is 'clinically proven' or one that is 'inspired by traditional/Ayurvedic wisdom'? Why? A: Strong_clinical
[SYNTHETIC] A) Switch to Cetaphil immediately — expert advice matters most. Dermatologists are my most trusted source
[SYNTHETIC] Trust the dermatologist since it's their field. Dermatologists are my most trusted source for personal care advice
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept endorsed by dermatologists or with clinical claims, this person would likely show immediate interest and trust, potentially overriding price concerns.

nodes.label: international-brands-signal-quality-over-indian
nodes.description: Consistently associates international brands with better quality than Indian brands, viewing Indian brands as cheap production with less confidence.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] International brands signal better quality than Indian brands. Indian brands are known for cheap production, which gives me less confidence to use them
[SYNTHETIC] The 'Imported from Korea' at ₹449. International brands signal better quality to me than Indian brands
[SYNTHETIC] Most premium to least: Forest Essentials, The Body Shop, Dove, Nivea, Fiama, mCaffeine, Lux. International brands signal better quality to me than Indian brands
[SYNTHETIC] International brands: 6 points... Legacy Indian brands: 1 point
nodes.predictive_value: When shown two identical body wash formulations, one positioned as international and one as Indian-made, this person would likely choose the international option even at a higher price point.

nodes.label: natural-claims-are-marketing-gimmicks
nodes.description: Dismisses natural and Ayurvedic claims as marketing tactics, showing 0% willingness to pay premium for such positioning.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] These claims of natural and sustainable are a gimmick to some extent. Those products also contain chemicals, and brands not claiming natural aren't giving you toxic stuff either
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? A: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0','Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0'}
[SYNTHETIC] I already think natural claims are gimmicks anyway
[SYNTHETIC] Skip it as too educational. I don't care much about natural vs organic certifications - these are mostly marketing gimmicks anyway
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept heavily emphasizing natural or Ayurvedic ingredients, this person would likely be skeptical and focus instead on clinical efficacy or price value.

nodes.label: comfortable-trying-new-brands-with-low-risk
nodes.description: Rates comfort with new brands as 4/5 and frequently experiments, especially when there's minimal financial risk or good deals.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 4
[REAL] I don't have nuanced body wash requirements; pretty much any product works
[SYNTHETIC] A) Give it a shot — one bad experience shouldn't stop you. I'm generally comfortable trying new brands
[SYNTHETIC] Free samples are always worth trying, and if it's personalized for my skin type, even better
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash brand with a trial offer or significant discount, this person would likely be willing to experiment because they're comfortable with product switching and don't have rigid requirements.

nodes.label: star-ratings-over-detailed-reviews
nodes.description: Prioritizes aggregate star ratings and review volume over reading detailed individual reviews or ingredient analysis.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ['price','brand_name','star_rating']
[SYNTHETIC] The one with 12,000+ ratings and 4.3 stars. Higher volume of reviews gives me more confidence than just 85 reviews, even if the rating is slightly lower
[SYNTHETIC] Star ratings are one of the first things I look at when shopping online, and volume of reviews gives me more confidence
[SYNTHETIC] Trust them anyway since the rating is high. I don't spend much time analyzing review authenticity
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash product page, this person would likely make purchase decisions based on the star rating and number of reviews rather than reading detailed feedback or ingredient lists.

nodes.label: avoids-bar-soap-for-hygiene-reasons
nodes.description: Specifically switched from bar soap to body wash due to contamination concerns in shared bathrooms, preferring individual bottles.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Currently use body wash. Stopped using bar soap since childhood – prefer a product untouched by others. In shared bathrooms, bar soap gets contaminated, but body wash stays pure for next use
[SYNTHETIC] I stopped using bar soap since childhood and prefer the hygiene of individual body wash bottles
[SYNTHETIC] C) Not try — I don't trust the hygiene of bulk dispensers. Shared dispensers for body wash seem unsanitary
[SYNTHETIC] B) Order a body wash on Blinkit/Zepto for quick delivery. I stopped using bar soap since childhood
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts emphasizing hygiene or individual packaging, this person would likely respond positively, while bulk or shared dispensing formats would be immediately rejected.

nodes.label: clinical-claims-over-traditional-wisdom
nodes.description: Strongly prefers clinically proven formulations over traditional or Ayurvedic approaches, willing to pay 10-20% premium for lab-tested products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you prefer a product that is 'clinically proven' or one that is 'inspired by traditional/Ayurvedic wisdom'? Why? A: Strong_clinical
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? A: {'Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'10_20'}
[SYNTHETIC] The clinical claim sounds more credible than a blog post, even without detailed methodology
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer clinically proven ingredients over traditional ones, and ingredients like niacinamide sound more effective
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept with clinical testing data or dermatologist validation, this person would likely be willing to pay a modest premium and show higher purchase intent than for traditional ingredient stories.

nodes.label: friends-family-recommendations-rank-high
nodes.description: Rates friends and family as second-highest trust source (4/5) after dermatologists, often following their specific product suggestions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {'Friends and family':4}
[SYNTHETIC] Ask them what they're using and buy the same one. If it's working for someone close to me, it's worth trying
[SYNTHETIC] The research-oriented friend's advice carries more weight
[SYNTHETIC] Family relationships matter more than brand preferences
nodes.predictive_value: When shown testimonials or word-of-mouth campaigns featuring real people's recommendations, this person would likely be more influenced than by celebrity endorsements or influencer content.

nodes.label: ignores-ingredient-lists-trusts-brands
nodes.description: Doesn't pay attention to detailed ingredient analysis, trusting that decent brands won't provide harmful products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {'Claims written on the product packaging':1}
[SYNTHETIC] Skip the ingredient list entirely. I don't understand most chemicals and it doesn't really matter to me - if the brand is decent, the product should be safe
[SYNTHETIC] Continue using it since it hasn't caused issues. If I've used it for a year without problems, that's better proof than some app's rating system
[SYNTHETIC] I wouldn't notice or understand the discrepancy. I don't pay much attention to ingredient lists
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept with detailed ingredient breakdowns or 'clean beauty' positioning, this person would likely skip over these details and focus instead on brand reputation and price.

nodes.label: oil-to-foam-texture-preference
nodes.description: Specifically prefers oil-to-foam texture in body wash, finding it more appealing than standard gel or cream formulations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Oil_to_foam
[SYNTHETIC] The oil-to-foam formula with vetiver scent. I prefer oil-to-foam texture and trying a completely new scent could be interesting
[REAL] The amount of foam it creates can be improved. It feels very liquidy and does not convert into a foam very easily
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts emphasizing rich lather or oil-to-foam transformation, this person would likely show higher interest than for standard gel formulations.

nodes.label: half-day-fragrance-duration-expectation
nodes.description: Expects body wash fragrance to last half a day on skin, viewing this as the ideal duration for social perception benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Half_day
[REAL] A fragrance feels special if it lasts longer and signals freshly bathed
[SYNTHETIC] How others perceive me matters, and a wedding is exactly the occasion where lasting fragrance is important
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts promising long-lasting fragrance or scent technology, this person would likely be attracted to claims about extended fragrance duration matching their half-day expectation.

nodes.label: fresh-citrus-ocean-fragrance-preference
nodes.description: Gravitates toward fresh citrus and ocean/aquatic fragrance families over floral, woody, or oriental scents.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Fresh_citrus,ocean_aquatic
[REAL] The fragrance should be natural lime flavor... The packaging needs to be lively and inviting
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with fresh citrus or ocean-inspired fragrances, this person would likely show immediate scent preference over floral or musky alternatives.

nodes.label: playful-packaging-over-minimalist
nodes.description: Prefers playful packaging design over minimalist or clinical aesthetics, wanting products that feel lively and inviting.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Playful
[REAL] The packaging needs to be lively and inviting
[SYNTHETIC] Brand B with the innovative micellar technology. I prefer newer, more advanced formulations over traditional brands, and the beauty award gives it credibility
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with vibrant, playful packaging versus sleek minimalist designs, this person would likely be drawn to the more colorful and energetic visual presentation.

nodes.label: dryness-skin-concern-awareness
nodes.description: Identifies dryness as their specific skin concern and expects body wash to address this issue complementarily.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Dryness
[REAL] Benefits have to be complimentary to how my skin feels. If my skin feels dry, then it needs to make me not feel dry
[REAL] How much attention do you pay to how your skin looks and feels on a daily basis? A: Notice_problems
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts specifically targeting dry skin or emphasizing moisturizing benefits, this person would likely show higher interest than for general cleansing or other skin benefits.

nodes.label: switches-for-skin-problems-doctor-recommendations-price
nodes.description: Would switch body wash brands primarily for skin problems, doctor recommendations, or cheaper alternatives, in that priority order.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ['skin_problem','doctor_rec','cheaper']
[SYNTHETIC] Since I notice skin problems and they're one of my top reasons to switch products, I wouldn't continue using something that causes dryness
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash marketing emphasizing problem-solving for skin issues or featuring medical professional endorsements, this person would likely be highly motivated to consider switching from their current product.

nodes.label: low-involvement-product-category-mindset
nodes.description: Views body wash as a low-involvement category where most products work adequately, not requiring sophisticated analysis or strong preferences.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] I don't have nuanced body wash requirements; pretty much any product works
[REAL] I was satisfied because I don't have nuanced body wash requirements; pretty much any product works
[SYNTHETIC] I'm not particular about body wash requirements - any decent product works for me, so why spend more?
[SYNTHETIC] I don't have very specific requirements - pretty much any body wash works for me
nodes.predictive_value: When shown complex body wash concepts with multiple benefits or sophisticated positioning, this person would likely prefer simpler, straightforward products that focus on basic cleansing and fragrance rather than advanced features.

nodes.label: social-media-ads-low-trust-but-discount-responsive
nodes.description: Rates Instagram/YouTube influencers low for trust (2/5) but responds to social media ads when they offer significant discounts.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you trust the following sources for personal care product information? Rate each 1-5: A: {'Instagram or YouTube influencers':2}
[REAL] I saw some Instagram ads with some recognizable people, and the price point was very low... I might have bought a product or two this way
[SYNTHETIC] B) Ad 2 — the discount is tempting enough to try something new. ₹75 off is significant savings
[REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: A: Expert_review,discount
nodes.predictive_value: When shown social media advertising for body wash, this person would likely ignore influencer endorsements but respond strongly to discount-focused ads, especially those offering substantial price reductions.

nodes.label: amazon-star-ratings-shopping-behavior
nodes.description: Relies heavily on Amazon's rating system and review volume as primary decision-making tools when shopping online.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ['price','brand_name','star_rating']
[SYNTHETIC] Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews. The larger sample size makes the rating more reliable
[SYNTHETIC] 4.3 stars from 12,000 reviews, 'Dermatologically Tested' claim, 'Amazon's Choice' badge
[SYNTHETIC] Amazon link. More comfortable with Amazon's return policy and delivery system
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash options across different platforms, this person would likely gravitate toward Amazon listings with high star ratings and substantial review counts, using these metrics as primary quality indicators.

nodes.label: money-back-guarantees-remove-purchase-hesitation
nodes.description: Responds very positively to satisfaction guarantees and money-back offers, viewing them as risk removal for trying new products.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] A) Yes — the guarantee removes my risk, I'll try it. The return policy makes the ₹150 premium feel safer to experiment with
[SYNTHETIC] A) Completely remove my hesitation to try them. This guarantee eliminates all risk and shows confidence in their product
[SYNTHETIC] A) Definitely make me try a brand I'd otherwise skip. The guarantee removes all financial risk
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with strong satisfaction guarantees or trial policies, this person would likely overcome price objections and be willing to try premium or unfamiliar brands they would otherwise avoid.

nodes.label: bulk-deals-attract-despite-single-need
nodes.description: Responds positively to bulk purchase deals and combo offers even when only needing one item, motivated by savings potential.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] B) Buy the combo to save money even though you don't need the second one yet. Saving ₹149 is worth it, and I'll eventually use the second bottle anyway
[SYNTHETIC] A) Buy the larger size - better value per ml. Even though it's a bigger upfront cost, I'll use it eventually and save money in the long run
[SYNTHETIC] ₹399 for ₹700+ worth of products is a great deal, and I'm comfortable trying random body washes
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash bundle offers or bulk purchase deals, this person would likely be attracted to the savings opportunity and willing to buy more than immediately needed to capture better per-unit pricing.

nodes.label: reddit-community-recommendations-trusted
nodes.description: Views Reddit community discussions as highly credible sources, trusting honest user experiences over branded content.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] A) Order immediately — community recommendations are gold. Reddit users give honest reviews without brand bias, so 8/10 positive reviews is very convincing
[SYNTHETIC] A) The Reddit-recommended one. Detailed analysis and multiple user experiences are more trustworthy than paid influencer partnerships
[SYNTHETIC] The Reddit thread with real photos. Seeing actual results from someone with similar skin concerns is more convincing than brand claims
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts that have gained organic traction in online communities or feature real user testimonials, this person would likely show higher trust and purchase intent than for traditional advertising approaches.

nodes.label: immediate-cashback-over-loyalty-points
nodes.description: Prefers immediate cashback and direct discounts over loyalty programs or points-based reward systems.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Brand B with ₹100 cashback. I only need one bottle and immediate cashback is better than extra products I don't need right now
[SYNTHETIC] A) Use SuperCoins — they're sitting idle anyway. Since they have no other immediate use, might as well use them to save ₹100
[SYNTHETIC] A) Buy from the cashback store - effectively ₹99 next time. I use quick commerce regularly, so I'd definitely shop there again
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash promotional offers, this person would likely prefer immediate price reductions or instant cashback over accumulating points or future benefits, favoring transparent value delivery.

nodes.label: social-validation-drives-repurchase-decisions
nodes.description: Positive comments from others about their fragrance or appearance strongly reinforce product loyalty and repurchase behavior.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] A) I'd definitely repurchase the same product — the compliment reinforces my choice. Since I care about how others perceive me, positive feedback would make me stick with that brand
[SYNTHETIC] A) I feel validated and more loyal to the brand
[REAL] The only reason I would pay 2x my current price is if people notice that I have changed body washes and they are impressed by the fragrance
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts emphasizing social recognition or featuring testimonials about compliments received, this person would likely be highly motivated because external validation directly reinforces their purchase satisfaction.

nodes.label: platform-loyalty-over-brand-website-direct

[SYNTHETIC] I prefer sticking to Amazon and quick commerce apps I trust rather than ordering from brand websites

nodes.label: trending-popularity-influences-trial-decisions
nodes.description: Responds positively to social proof signals like bestseller tags, trending status, and high sales volumes when considering new products.

[SYNTHETIC] A) More confident in my choice — clearly it's popular for a reason. The widespread marketing would validate that it's a good product
[SYNTHETIC] B) Curious enough to click and read more
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with strong social proof indicators like 'bestseller' status or trending metrics, this person would likely show increased interest and willingness to try compared to products without popularity signals.

nodes.label: special-occasions-justify-premium-purchases

[SYNTHETIC] A) Yes — first impressions matter and I want to smell great

nodes.label: convenience-over-cost-optimization

[SYNTHETIC] Helpful and convenient. I often forget to reorder until I'm completely out, so this kind of reminder would actually be useful
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash purchasing options with different convenience levels, this person would likely choose faster, easier options even at slightly higher prices rather than optimizing for maximum savings.

nodes.label: rigid-200-rupee-budget-ceiling

nodes.label: multi-benefit-time-saver-seeker

nodes.label: foam-texture-quality-focused

nodes.label: claim-skeptic-needs-validation

nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts that require extended use time or special routines, this person would likely reject them in favor of quick, efficient options.

nodes.label: value-maximizer-over-premium-features

nodes.label: individual-bundle-rejector
nodes.description: Prefers individual products over bundles or couples packages, wanting flexibility in purchase decisions.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts sold as bundles or multi-product packages, this person would likely prefer individual purchase options to avoid complexity and higher costs.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts requiring personal data input or photo sharing, this person would likely reject these features as unnecessary complications.

nodes.label: convenience-functionality-balancer
nodes.description: Seeks convenient products but balances this with practical functionality needs.

nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts emphasizing convenience features that align with practical benefits, this person would likely show strong interest in products that genuinely simplify their routine.

links.mechanism: Their strict price ceiling makes them highly sensitive to deals and promotions, leading them to switch brands whenever better pricing appears rather than maintaining loyalty to higher-priced options.

Because this person views fragrance as a tool for positive social perception, compliments about their scent directly validate their product choice and strongly motivate continued use of the same product.

Because fragrance directly impacts how others perceive them, high-stakes social situations where perception matters most motivate them to invest in premium products they would normally avoid due to price sensitivity.
Their heavy reliance on instant delivery services demonstrates a consistent pattern of prioritizing immediate availability and ease of purchase over price optimization across shopping decisions.

Their high trust in medical professionals naturally aligns with preference for scientifically-backed formulations, creating consistent decision-making criteria across information sources and product positioning.

Their preference for international brands aligns with skepticism toward traditional/natural positioning, as they associate quality with clinical formulations rather than heritage or natural ingredient stories.
Both traits reflect a preference for simplified decision-making shortcuts rather than detailed analysis, relying on aggregate signals (ratings, brand reputation) over granular information.
Because they view body wash as a category where most products work adequately, they feel safe experimenting with new brands since the risk of dissatisfaction is perceived as low.
Both traits show preference for transparent, immediate value delivery over complex or delayed benefits, whether in promotional messaging or reward structures.
Risk-removal mechanisms like guarantees amplify their existing openness to experimentation by addressing their main concern about trying unfamiliar products - potential financial loss.
The strict budget limit prevents them from purchasing innovative products they find interesting, creating a pattern where they appreciate novelty but can't act on it due to pricing.
Their strict budget limit makes bundles and couples packages financially inaccessible, leading them to prefer individual products that fit within their price range.
Their desire for products that combine multiple benefits aligns with their preference for quick routines, as multi-functional products reduce the time and steps needed.
Their rejection of complicated concepts reinforces their preference for quick routines, as complex positioning often implies more elaborate usage processes.
Their skepticism toward new claims makes them more likely to stick with established brands they already trust rather than risk trying unproven products.
Their focus on value for money reinforces their strict budget ceiling, as they refuse to pay premiums even for features they might want.
Their desire for moisturizing benefits makes them appreciate products that combine cleansing and hydration, eliminating the need for separate body lotion application.
Their discomfort with technology features reinforces their preference for simple positioning, as they want straightforward products without complicated digital interactions.
